hypotheses.label: n-gram path-tuple retention lets naive_ngram4 reach deeper sequential-decode path states that plain edge coverage collapses and discards

params.depth_proxy: max sequential-decode iteration / cascade-stage index reached at the blocker function (e.g. UTF16/UTF8 decode-loop iteration count, encoding-detection if-cascade stage, content-dispatch fan depth) measured by instrumented replay of the W (naive_ngram4-resolving) vs L (naive-blocking) seed sets through FUZZ_BIN

hypotheses.prediction: The naive_ngram4-resolving (winner) seed set reaches a strictly deeper sequential-decode path state at the blocker than the naive-blocking (loser) seed set: winner_depth > loser_depth (depth_lift >= 1.5). This is discriminating because an alternative where naive simply lacks the right LITERAL bytes (an I2S/value-substitution story, not a coverage-retention story) would predict the loser seeds reaching the SAME cascade depth but failing the terminal byte comparison (depth_lift ~ 1.0) — i.e. the gate fails at the literal, not for lack of path depth. n-gram retention predicts the loser is stalled SHALLOW (its near-miss intermediate seeds were discarded), so the loser never even arrives at the deep decode stage.

notes: MERGE: all 4 branches (libxml2 6325 UTF16LE surrogate decode, 6359 UTF-8 BOM detection cascade, 6835 content-dispatch CDATA fan, 6887 overlong-3-byte UTF-8 decode) collapse to ONE hypothesis — their mechanism_summaries describe the identical effect (4-edge n-gram tuples turn sequential cascade/decode states into fresh map buckets, retaining near-miss seeds plain single-edge coverage drops). No real-campaign measurement could separate 'surrogate decode' from 'BOM cascade' from 'CDATA fan' as DISTINCT mechanisms — they differ only in WHICH sequential structure is being retained, not in the retention effect — so keeping them apart would be fuzzy, not discriminable. G1: the depth_lift test discriminates ngram-retention (loser stalls shallow) from a literal-substitution alternative (loser reaches same depth, fails at the byte). G2 (independence): the measurement is a depth-of-reach over the seed sets, NOT a re-statement of the naive_ngram4-resolves-naive-blocks pattern that defined the shape — it asks WHY (path depth) rather than re-confirming the resolve outcome. DATA-REALITY CAVEAT (feasibility): registry data_realities state on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 — libxml2 is NOT among them, and ALL 4 branches here are libxml2. The depth_reach measurement therefore depends on the per-branch resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds being present in db/blockers.sqlite (the 286 reps carry them) and on instrumented replay through FUZZ_BIN rather than on a saved corpus_queue; if those seed sets are absent for a branch, that branch falls to fallback:inconclusive. UNBUILT TOOL: no registry tool measures sequential-decode depth_reach (joint_necessity measures struct size/token count, operand_enrichment measures byte-frequency at fixed offsets — neither captures path depth), so registry_tool=null and depth_reach (source=seed_set, compute=depth_reach, operand=sequential_path_depth, unit=per_branch) is a NEW descriptor for the orchestrator to build.
